title: Battlecards · Executive summary · 21 competitors at a glance
summary:
One-screen orientation for board, investor, and CHRO readers — three sentences per competitor: positioning, recent move, and the seam against Stratum. Long battlecards stay at /intel/eglin/competitors/ for the deep read.

— TOP TRANCHE —

Workday — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/workday.html
The enterprise HCM incumbent — $8.4B revenue, 60% of the Fortune 500, default for the 5,000+ employee tier. Illuminate agents and the Agent System of Record with MCP support shipped GA in 2026, so the 'Workday has no AI' framing is wrong; what they do have is Mobley v. Workday, an ADEA collective action where their own filings disclosed 1.1 billion application rejections. Stratum doesn't replace Workday at the 100k tier — we sit on top of Workday Core HCM as best-of-breed Recruiting, with three sharp seams: published MCP openness, operational governance artifacts (eval set, ledger, refusals), and recruiter-console throughput where Workday's UX complaints are decades-old.

Rippling — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/rippling.html
The most strategically interesting non-incumbent in HCM — $1B+ ARR growing 78% YoY on a genuinely unified HR/IT/Finance data core competitors can't replicate without forklifting their object models. Rippling AI's March 2026 launch was Parker Conrad's biggest in company history, but it's a deterministic code-generating surface inside a closed wall, and the Deel espionage litigation (Rippling is the plaintiff; bank records implicate Deel's COO) consumes executive bandwidth. Stratum's seam: MCP openness vs Rippling's closed AI surface, governance as published artifact, and recruiting-first depth where Rippling Recruiting is a module not the focus.

Eightfold — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/eightfold.html
The original 'talent intelligence' AI company — Deep Matching on a billion-career-data-point corpus, with one of only two third-party-audited matching models in the watch-list (BABL AI, 2026, passed NYC AEDT). The bias-audit framing that worked against Greenhouse does not work here; the central buyer-trust problem is matching accuracy itself, with G2 reviewers repeatedly describing erroneous matches, inferred skills, and resume-parsing failures. Stratum wins on matching transparency (the people-graph + eval-set is more inspectable than Eightfold's audited-but-opaque model), MCP openness, and recruiter override UX when the AI is wrong.

iCIMS — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/icims.html
The 25-year-old enterprise ATS, ~6,000 customers, Vista Equity-owned since 2018 — running a deliberate AI repositioning with the Sourcing Agent (Fall 2025) and Frontline AI for high-volume hiring (Spring 2026). The AI cadence is real and accelerating, but it sits on top of 25 years of UI accretion that reviewers consistently call 'powerful but dated.' Modern recruiter UX is the single most exploitable wedge — Tessera's console clarity against any 20-year-old ATS — plus MCP openness against iCIMS's closed agent-network and the absent third-party bias audit.

SmartRecruiters — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/smartrecruiters.html
Acquired by SAP in September 2025, now the recruiting-experience layer of SuccessFactors with single-sign-on to Joule generative AI. The April 2026 'autonomous talent acquisition' announcement (agentic interviewing, agentic CRM, fraud detection) is the most aggressive AI claim in the watch-list — the supporting architecture document is not yet public, and the Winston-Joule integration ships H2 2026. SmartRecruiters' value increases inside SAP and decreases outside it; Stratum is HCM-agnostic, MCP-native, and lives on top of any record system.

Lever — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/lever.html
The canonical 'modern ATS+CRM' of the 2015-2020 era — clean UI, recruiter-friendly, strong nurture-campaign CRM. The Employ Inc. (K1 Investment Management) 2022 acquisition has visibly slowed product cadence; G2 reviewers explicitly cite undelivered roadmap requests and AI features that 'lag competitors.' This is a slow-moving target where Stratum's wedge is simply doing the work — AI cadence, MCP openness, governance artifacts, pricing transparency.

— MID-MARKET HRIS / ENGAGEMENT / PERFORMANCE —

HiBob — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/hibob.html
The most culturally-distinct HRIS in the watch-list — 'Bob' is the HRIS designed for the people who use it, not the people who buy it. Strong on engagement, kudos, clubs, and surveys; weak on payroll globalization, reporting depth, and AI (HiBob itself acknowledges 'the AI features are not quite where I would like them to be'). The 2026 Workforce Planning API is a real integration play — Stratum Recruiter on top of HiBob HRIS via the people-graph is the realistic motion, not displacement.

BambooHR — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/bamboohr.html
The default SMB HRIS in the US, ~34,000 customers, the most-named platform when an SMB asks 'what HR software do I buy.' Deliberately modest AI posture (Ask BambooHR for routine questions) — fits the buyer who doesn't want experimental AI. Not Stratum's competitive set; the move is partnership and an upgrade path for BambooHR alumni who grow past 500 employees and start asking about agentic recruiting.

Lattice — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/lattice.html
The most disciplined strategic move in HCM in years: launched an HRIS and payroll, found the unit economics unworkable, publicly announced sunsetting both by July 2026 to refocus on performance, engagement, growth, and the AI Agent. The product on the focused surface is strong; reviewer complaints are about navigation complexity, not AI quality. Pure complementary lane for Stratum — Stratum Recruiter + Lattice Performance via the people-graph, with the July 2026 migration as a sales-cycle trigger.

Culture Amp — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/culture-amp.html
The engagement-survey category leader, benchmarking against 1B+ survey responses. AI is survey-analytics focused (theme detection, sentiment flagging, coaching chatbot) — conservative on agents, which fits the buyer who values benchmarking integrity over experimental features. Not a direct competitor — Stratum Recruiter brings new hires into Culture Amp engagement-tracking via the people-graph; build the integration, not the comparison.

15Five — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/15five.html
The manager-effectiveness platform — weekly check-ins, 1:1s, OKRs, with two scoped, named AI products: Kona (joins Zoom/Meet/Teams 1:1s, captures wins/blockers/actions) and AMAYA (plain-language analytics across performance, engagement, retention, comp). Disciplined AI productization — scoped, named, operationally credible. Complementary partner: new hires from Stratum Recruiter flow into 15Five manager-coaching workflows through the graph.

— PAYROLL HEAVYWEIGHTS —

ADP — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/adp.html
The payroll incumbent — ~$20B revenue, processes payroll for roughly one in six US workers, with ADP Lyric HCM winning recent large enterprise deals. The March 2026 ADP Marketplace AI Agents launch is the most strategically interesting partner-distribution event in HCM this year — ADP is positioning itself as the platform where customers consume third-party AI agents. The move is to be one of those agents: Stratum Recruiter as a curated agent on ADP Marketplace, integrated with their payroll-of-record base.

UKG — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/ukg.html
The Kronos + Ultimate Software merger (2020), workforce-management-led — deep in time, attendance, scheduling, and hourly-compliance for retail, hospitality, healthcare, manufacturing. UKG Bryte AI is embedded across the surface but the focus is frontline workforce, not specialized recruiting. Mostly partnership territory — Stratum Recruiter on top of UKG payroll-of-record via integration.

Paylocity — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/paylocity.html
The mid-market HCM/payroll vendor that has built durable customer satisfaction (G2 4.5/5 across ~5,000 reviews) on customer-engagement motion and per-module pricing transparency relative to Paycom. AI is 'embedded' rather than an agent platform — the cadence trails the headline-AI competitors. Partnership lane: Paylocity customers who want best-in-class recruiting are the complement-sale.

Paycom — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/paycom.html
Distinguished by Beti, a self-service paycheck-verification product that reduces payroll corrections by 80%+ — a genuine product innovation Paylocity has no equivalent for. IWant is the command-driven AI engine on a single-database architecture; the buyer-experience tax is 15-30% mandatory implementation fees, rigid configurability, and mixed post-launch support. Investor coverage now asks whether Beti is a moat or a feature; Stratum's lane is partnership, not displacement.

— ADJACENT —

Deel — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/deel.html
The global-employment infrastructure leader — EOR, contractor management, payroll across 150+ countries, ~$12B valuation, Trustpilot 4.8/5. August 2025 Deel AI Workforce shipped a disciplined set of named agents (Hiring Guru, PTO Fairy, Border Buddy, Payroll Detective). Deel is the defendant in the Rippling espionage suit with active DOJ interest — Stratum's policy is not to weaponize this; the realistic motion is a partnership connector for cross-border recruiting (Stratum sources globally, Deel handles EOR conversion).

Gusto — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/gusto.html
The SMB payroll leader, ~300,000 customers concentrated below 100 employees, with a strong brand and an April 2026 '75 new features' release that adds S-corp election with AI advisory, ChatGPT/Claude/Slack workflows, Gus AI assistant, and a Money Dashboard. The Plus plan is increasingly a functional HRIS for the 10-100 range; Trustpilot 2.5/5 shows the SMB-trust hinge — users love the product when it works. Not a direct Stratum set; the lane is upgrade-path partnership for Gusto alumni growing past 100 employees.

Phenom — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/phenom.html
The Intelligent Talent Experience Management platform — end-to-end candidate sites, recruiter AI co-pilots, internal mobility, workforce planning, focused on the 1,000+ employee enterprise. Reviewers cite algorithm-quality concerns, new-release QA issues, and a 'US-centric view on data privacy' that creates global compliance gaps; bias-audit posture is thinner than Eightfold's BABL audit. Stratum's seams: matching transparency, MCP openness, published governance, and global compliance depth.

— ALREADY LIVE —

Ashby — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/ashby.html
The stronger AI competitor in the watch-list — ships features the market endorses, holds the only published third-party bias audit (FairNow), and customer roster (Cursor, OpenAI, Lovable, Shopify) is the AI category's buying signal. The May 2026 Ashby One release acquired Talent Llama (AI interviewer, owned not partnered), extended past the ATS boundary into onboarding with People Workflows, and opened Custom Agents and Ashby Assistant in beta — earlier-stage than the messaging implies. Stratum's narrowed wedges: MCP completeness today (Ashby's is 'upcoming,' ours has four servers shipping), continuous governance artifacts vs periodic audit, and recruiter-console clarity for the mid-market buyer who finds Ashby over-engineered.

Greenhouse — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/greenhouse.html
The tenured structured-hiring ATS — ~7,500 customers (Trivago, HubSpot, DoorDash, Lyft), the preferred choice for teams who prioritize hiring rigor over suite breadth. May 2026 Greenhouse MCP announcement is the most material AI-posture shift since the original feature wave; G2 reviewers note Ashby is 'miles ahead in AI and automation.' The 'governed' framing leans on language not yet evidenced by published technical documentation — Stratum has the MCP architecture, the operational governance artifacts, and the AI cadence Greenhouse is currently trying to reframe.

ClearCompany — https://mpaiva-cc.github.io/stratum-chairman-briefing/intel/eglin/competitors/clearcompany.html
Rebranded to 'ClearCo' on March 2026 with an 'AI- and agent-powered unified talent platform' positioning — the rebrand is real, the agent framework is 'next chapter' rather than shipped, and the AI feature list lacks beta/GA labels, named LLM providers, or any architecture document. No MCP, no bias audit, no AEDT page, no VPAT, no Responsible-AI statement; security posture is SOC 2 + GDPR + CCPA (category table-stakes). Not a head-to-head buyer-set conflict — useful contrast case for Compass and a preview of how tenured suites narrate the AI transition without underlying architectural proof.
